mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that has remained flat for six or more consecutive months after a tightening cycle signals the Fed has reached a terminal rate, reducing near-term discount-rate uncertainty and supporting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS plateau duration and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following plateaus are not significantly different from baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in housing starts exceeding 20% signals a contraction in residential investment that historically precedes broader demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1486k units, down sharply from 2005 peak above 2000k
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where HOUST YoY decline exceeds -20%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth and a rise in the unemployment rate jointly signal an inflection in labor market conditions that equity markets have not yet priced, producing negative forward 20-day returns because the two signals reinforce a genuine cyclical turn rather than noise in either series alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 1% AND UNRATE up >= 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce positive median 20-day forward SPX return, or the interaction model's out-of-sample Rank IC does not exceed the best single-series model's IC by any margin.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects continued expansion in real output and corporate revenue capacity, supporting short-horizon equity momentum through positive earnings revisions over the following trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive INDPRO prints are not statistically distinguishable from returns following negative prints (t-test p>0.10) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate has plateaued at a restrictive level while headline CPI growth decelerates, the market prices in an approaching easing cycle, and this specific late-tightening regime produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as forward-looking rate-cut expectations are built into valuations; outside this regime the same CPI deceleration has no consistent directional effect.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS flat for 5+ months AND CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth decelerating
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 201.7 with disinflation trend into late 2006
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening pause before pivot
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during this joint regime show negative mean return or Rank IC below 0.03 across rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in retail sales indicates resilient consumer demand, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 368861 million, consistent with steady consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS monthly growth and forward 5-day SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts combined with a still-elevated federal funds rate indicates that monetary policy remains restrictive even as the most rate-sensitive sector of the economy is contracting, creating a compounded demand-shock risk that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter beyond what either series predicts alone.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST declining while FEDFUNDS held at 5.25%
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy combined with housing downturn
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in over 50% of joint-trigger instances, or the two-series interaction model fails to outperform either single-series baseline by any Rank IC margin.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of 0.3 percentage points or more above its prior 3-month average constitutes an early recession warning signal that historically precedes equity market drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month once triggered.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 3-month average
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5%, near cycle lows as of Nov 2006
failure_condition: Following triggers, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling walk-forward test.
